content:
I have recently started sending patches (rfcs), and I noticed that the Submitting patches instructions in https://rust-for-linux.com/contributing produce

--to 'Miguel Ojeda <ojeda@kernel.org>' \
--to 'Alex Gaynor <alex.gaynor@gmail.com>' \
--cc 'Boqun Feng <boqun.feng@gmail.com>' \
--cc 'Gary Guo <gary@garyguo.net>' \
--cc 'Björn Roy Baron <bjorn3_gh@protonmail.com>' \
--cc 'Benno Lossin <benno.lossin@proton.me>' \
--cc 'Andreas Hindborg <a.hindborg@kernel.org>' \
--cc 'Alice Ryhl <aliceryhl@google.com>' \
--cc 'Trevor Gross <tmgross@umich.edu>' \
--cc 'rust-for-linux@vger.kernel.org' \

whereas the b4 prep --auto-to-cc command produces

    + To: Miguel Ojeda <ojeda@kernel.org>
    + To: Alex Gaynor <alex.gaynor@gmail.com>
    + To: Boqun Feng <boqun.feng@gmail.com>
    + To: Gary Guo <gary@garyguo.net>
    + To: Björn Roy Baron <bjorn3_gh@protonmail.com>
    + To: Benno Lossin <benno.lossin@proton.me>
    + To: Andreas Hindborg <a.hindborg@kernel.org>
    + To: Alice Ryhl <aliceryhl@google.com>
    + To: Trevor Gross <tmgross@umich.edu>
    + Cc: rust-for-linux@vger.kernel.org produces

which has many Cc: switched to To:. I have been using the first set, is one preferred over the other ?

In general, it doesn't matter much, the "Submitting patches instructions" makes a bit more sense because I know there are people filtering "To:" and "Cc:" into different folders, and being on the "To:" list means "this needs reaction from me". But I don't think that's a hard requirement. Choose whatever makes sense to you ;-)

Yeah, there are many ways people send it, and it does not matter too much. Some even just To: a list and put in Cc: everyone else, though I would argue that is not great for the reason @Boqun Feng describes.

In the website I just used the style that I normally use, but if b4 picks a certain style, we could align with it too. Does b4 in the latest version use Cc for the Cc: by default or does it convert it to To:?

Boqun Feng said:

the "Submitting patches instructions" makes a bit more sense because I know there are people filtering "To:" and "Cc:" into different folders, and being on the "To:" list means "this needs reaction from me".

this was my thought process too

Miguel Ojeda said:

Does b4 in the latest version use Cc for the Cc: by default or does it convert it to To:?

I am not sure I understand the question, sorry
